notes: Evals for the Browzer /commit skill. Each prompt simulates a realistic moment in a Browzer monorepo session — the kind of thing a dev actually types right before shipping. The 'context' field describes the simulated repo state the runner should set up (mock staged diff + recent log + simulated outputs of the `<live_context>` doc-discovery probes) before invoking the skill. Assertions check the four things that matter: (1) Conventional Commits shape, (2) Browzer house style (correct scope, nested scopes, lowercase type), (3) the Browzer Co-Authored-By trailer is present with the noreply.github.com email, (4) the doc-sync pre-flight surfaces real candidates (cross-refs, nearest agent doc, backlog trackers) before composing the message, without fabricating files the probes did not return. Subjective body quality is left to the human review tab. Probe-simulation fields in context: `cross_ref_docs` (cross-ref probe output), `nearest_agent_docs` (per-directory walker output), `backlog_docs` (backlog/debt/plan probe output), `root_docs` (top-level doc probe output). The runner stubs these into the live_context block before invoking the skill.

evals.name: simple-fix-with-nested-scope
evals.prompt: I staged a one-line fix in apps/api/src/routes/documents.ts that returns `[]` instead of `null` when an org has no docs yet. Write me the commit message.

recent_log: f465614 refactor(web): rebuild auth pages around Monolithic Console vocabulary
75c4a51 fix(api/documents): return [] instead of null for empty doc lists
be0f47d fix(gateway): route /api/organization/* to apps/api

evals.expected_output: A Conventional Commit with type `fix`, scope `api/documents` (nested — matches house style), lowercase imperative subject, and the browzeremb Co-Authored-By trailer. No body required for a one-liner, but if present should explain WHY not WHAT.

assertions.text: Subject starts with `fix(api/documents):` (nested scope matching the actual file path and existing house-style commits)
Subject is lowercase imperative mood (not past tense like 'fixed' or 'returned')
Subject has no trailing period
Message ends with a Co-Authored-By trailer crediting browzeremb via the users.noreply.github.com email

evals.name: feat-with-issue-ref-via-gh
evals.prompt: I'm closing issue #42 on github.com/browzeremb/browzer — it's about exposing sourceHash on the document list endpoint. Staged diff touches packages/core/src/store/documents.ts and apps/api/src/routes/documents.ts. `gh` is available. Write the commit message; grab the issue title from gh first so we get the phrasing right.

context.staged_diff:
diff --git a/packages/core/src/store/documents.ts ...
+  sourceHash: doc.sourceHash,
diff --git a/apps/api/src/routes/documents.ts ...
+  sourceHash: d.sourceHash,
recent_log: 4586f0d feat(core): expose document sourceHash in listDocumentsInWorkspace
0d29fda feat(core): add contentFingerprint property and store methods

evals.expected_output: A `feat` commit. Scope should be the primary area — likely `core` given recent history (the api-side change just plumbs through). Includes a `Fixes: #42` or `Closes: #42` footer, then the browzeremb trailer. The skill should MENTION it used `gh issue view 42` to confirm the title (or note it in its response text).

assertions.text: Subject uses `feat` type with a Browzer-valid scope (`core`, `api`, or nested)
Includes a GitHub-style issue footer (Fixes: #42, Closes: #42, or Refs: #42)
Co-Authored-By browzeremb trailer is the last non-empty line of the message
Response text (not the commit message itself) mentions using `gh` to fetch the issue title

evals.name: breaking-change-v1-endpoint-removal
evals.prompt: Dropping the unauthenticated /v1/ask endpoint in apps/api. All traffic must now go through AUTH_SERVICE_URL with a bearer token. Give me the commit message — this is a breaking change and downstream clients will be affected.

recent_log: f465614 refactor(web): rebuild auth pages around Monolithic Console vocabulary
aeff4ac feat(web): add /docs page covering HTTP API, CLI, and skills

evals.expected_output: A breaking-change `feat` commit. Uses `!` after the scope (`feat(api)!:`) AND a `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer explaining the migration path (auth token required). Body explains the why. Browzer trailer at the end.

assertions.text: Subject uses the `!` breaking-change marker: feat(api)!:
Contains an uppercase BREAKING CHANGE footer (token must be uppercase per spec)
BREAKING CHANGE footer mentions the migration path (auth / bearer / API key)
Co-Authored-By browzeremb trailer present with noreply.github.com email

evals.name: type-disambiguation-refactor-vs-chore
evals.prompt: I moved the sensitive-filter logic from packages/shared/src/index.ts into a dedicated packages/shared/src/sensitive-filter.ts file and re-exported it. No behavior change, no new features, no bug fixes — pure internal reshuffling. What's the right commit?

recent_log: 48b0d5d refactor(web): extract shared UI primitives from homepage
c03b7cf refactor(api): address CodeRabbit review on parse handler gates

evals.expected_output: `refactor(shared): ...` — not `chore`, because this is a code restructure with no behavior change, and not `feat` because no new capability. Scope is `shared` (matches the monorepo package). Body should note 'no behavior change' or similar to head off reviewer questions.

assertions.text: Uses `refactor` type — not `chore`, not `feat`, not `fix`
Scope is `shared` (the actual package being touched)
Co-Authored-By browzeremb trailer present

evals.name: no-gh-no-glab-fallback
evals.prompt: Commit a fix to apps/gateway — added a route rule so /grafana/* proxies to the Grafana service. Neither `gh` nor `glab` is installed on this machine. Don't block on missing CLIs.

recent_log: be0f47d fix(gateway): route /api/organization/* to apps/api
ef45fe6 merge: gateway routing /api/organization (fix #1)

evals.expected_output: A clean `feat(gateway):` or `fix(gateway):` commit. The skill must NOT refuse or stall because forge CLI is missing — it should commit with plain git and, if any forge metadata would have been useful, flag that in its response text.

assertions.text: Uses `gateway` scope
Co-Authored-By browzeremb trailer present
Skill response text acknowledges missing forge CLI without blocking the commit (or just proceeds silently — either is acceptable, what's NOT acceptable is refusing to commit)

evals.name: multi-area-split-recommendation
evals.prompt: I have changes staged across apps/auth (new route), apps/web (new signup page), AND packages/db (new migration for org_plan column). Write me ONE commit for all of it.

recent_log: 64b42d9 fix(auth): email verification, OAuth org auto-provisioning, Resend
aeff4ac feat(web): add /docs page covering HTTP API, CLI, and skills

evals.expected_output: The skill should push back and recommend splitting into multiple commits (the 'When a change fits two types, split the commit' rule + the scope guidance). If the user insists on one, omit the scope rather than listing three. Either way, `Co-Authored-By: browzeremb` trailer is present.

assertions.text: Either recommends splitting the commit OR produces a single commit without a multi-scope list (no `feat(auth,web,db)`, no `feat(auth/web):`)
Does NOT produce a comma-separated or slash-joined scope list
Co-Authored-By browzeremb trailer present on whatever message(s) it writes

evals.name: revert-with-hash-refs
evals.prompt: Revert commit 676104e — it broke the rag embedding endpoint. Also pulls along a2215868 which was a follow-up. Write the revert message.

recent_log: 676104e feat(rag): switch to new ONNX runtime
a215868 fix(rag): tweak batch size for new runtime

evals.expected_output: `revert:` type (no scope — or scope `rag` is acceptable since the repo has precedent). Includes a `Refs:` footer listing both SHAs. Browzer trailer last.

assertions.text: Uses `revert` as the type
Includes a Refs footer with at least one of the SHAs (676104e or a215868)
Co-Authored-By browzeremb trailer present

evals.name: ci-change-not-chore
evals.prompt: Tweaked .github/workflows/ci.yml to skip the integration-tests job on docs-only PRs. What's the commit?

recent_log: dc8b67d ci: make security audit non-blocking during npm endpoint deprecation
3d724a6 chore(ci): trigger run after billing unblock

evals.expected_output: Type is `ci` (not `chore` — CI workflow changes have their own type in the spec AND in this repo's history). No scope required, or optional scope describing the job. Browzer trailer.

assertions.text: Uses `ci` type (not `chore`, not `build`)
Co-Authored-By browzeremb trailer present

evals.name: doc-sync-flags-cross-ref
evals.prompt: I staged a fix in packages/shared/src/sensitive-filter.ts — the isSensitive check was letting `.pem` files through when the extension was uppercase. Commit this for me.

recent_log: f465614 refactor(web): rebuild auth pages around Monolithic Console vocabulary
75c4a51 fix(api/documents): return [] instead of null for empty doc lists

evals.expected_output: Before committing, the skill should surface the doc-sync candidates the probes returned — specifically `docs/SECURITY.md` and `packages/shared/CLAUDE.md` (both cross-ref the staged file) — and ask the user whether to stage doc updates too, or commit code-only. If the user doesn't answer, it's fine to proceed with the code-only commit; the key behavior is that the candidates were surfaced, not that the commit was blocked.

assertions.text: Response text surfaces at least one of the cross-ref candidates by path before committing
Response text frames the doc candidates as a question or recommendation — not a silent assumption
Eventually produces a Conventional Commit (fix or refactor) with a scope in {shared, compliance, security}
Co-Authored-By browzeremb trailer present on the commit message
Does NOT fabricate doc paths the probes didn't return (no invented `docs/sensitive-filter.md` or similar)

evals.name: doc-sync-flags-backlog-item
evals.prompt: Closing debt B12 — the per-org vector index was rejecting better-auth `org_<uuid>` identifiers because the regex didn't allow underscores. Fixed in packages/core/src/store/per-org-vector-index.ts. Commit this.

recent_log: 49077fb docs(debts): record B8 / G15.8 / B10 from workflow skills validation
6e2dd80 fix(core): accept better-auth org plugin `org_<uuid>` ids in per-org indexes

evals.expected_output: The user explicitly named a tracked debt (B12). The skill must surface `docs/TECHNICAL_DEBTS.md` as a candidate for a paired update — marking B12 resolved with a dated note or commit SHA. Should ask the user whether to include the debt-log update in the same commit or split it. Commit body should use the `fix(core)` scope consistent with recent history.

assertions.text: Response text mentions the backlog tracker (`docs/TECHNICAL_DEBTS.md` or `TECHNICAL_DEBTS`) as something to update
Response text references the specific debt ID the user mentioned (B12)
Commit message uses `fix(core):` scope
Co-Authored-By browzeremb trailer present

evals.name: doc-sync-empty-probes-no-fabrication
evals.prompt: Quick typo fix in apps/web/src/app/privacy-policy/page.tsx — 'recieve' → 'receive'. Commit it.

context.staged_diff:
diff --git a/apps/web/src/app/privacy-policy/page.tsx ...
-  We recieve data from our users
+  We receive data from our users
recent_log: f465614 refactor(web): rebuild auth pages around Monolithic Console vocabulary

evals.expected_output: A tiny typo fix touches no architecture and has no cross-refs. The skill should NOT invent doc-sync work (no fake `docs/privacy.md` to update, no forced CLAUDE.md edit). Either skip the pre-flight silently or note it passes cleanly, then produce a short `fix(web):` commit.

assertions.text: Produces a short `fix(web)` commit — no body required for a typo
Does NOT fabricate doc paths that weren't in the probes
Does NOT push the user to update root CLAUDE.md for a typo (no architectural shift)
Co-Authored-By browzeremb trailer present

evals.name: doc-sync-user-says-just-commit
evals.prompt: I'll update the docs in a follow-up PR. Just commit the code for now: staged diff adds a new env var INTERNAL_SERVICE_SECRET_V2 to apps/api/src/env.ts and wires it into apps/api/src/middleware/internal-auth.ts.

context.staged_diff:
diff --git a/apps/api/src/env.ts ...
+INTERNAL_SERVICE_SECRET_V2: z.string().min(32),
diff --git a/apps/api/src/middleware/internal-auth.ts ...
+const secret = env.INTERNAL_SERVICE_SECRET_V2 ?? env.INTERNAL_SERVICE_SECRET;
recent_log: b4e24aa feat(api): add api-key audit log partitioned table

evals.expected_output: The user explicitly said 'just commit the code for now'. The skill may briefly note the doc candidates (one sentence max) so the user can find them later in the follow-up PR, but MUST NOT block, repeatedly push back, or litigate. Produces the `feat(api)` commit and proceeds.

assertions.text: Produces a `feat(api)` or `feat(api/...)` commit without getting stuck on doc-sync
Does NOT refuse or repeatedly insist on doc updates after the user said 'just commit'
assertions.pattern: (?is)(I really think you should|please (update|reconsider)|I must insist|cannot commit until|refuse to commit|blocking on)

assertions.text: Co-Authored-By browzeremb trailer present

evals.name: doc-sync-nearest-agent-for-env-var
evals.prompt: Added a new shared env var GRAFANA_UPSTREAM_URL to apps/gateway — it controls whether the gateway proxies /grafana/*. Commit this; make sure we capture the env var somewhere so the next person doesn't lose the context.

context.staged_diff:
diff --git a/apps/gateway/src/env.ts ...
+GRAFANA_UPSTREAM_URL: z.string().url().optional(),
diff --git a/apps/gateway/src/index.ts ...
+if (env.GRAFANA_UPSTREAM_URL) server.register(proxy, { upstream: env.GRAFANA_UPSTREAM_URL, prefix: '/grafana' });
recent_log: be0f47d fix(gateway): route /api/organization/* to apps/api

evals.expected_output: A new shared env var is an architectural signal. Even with no cross-refs, the skill should recommend adding the env var to the nearest agent doc (`apps/gateway/CLAUDE.md`) and — because the user called this 'shared' — the root `CLAUDE.md` env-var section too. The user's 'make sure we capture the env var somewhere' is a direct request to update docs, so the skill should propose doing so before or alongside the commit.

assertions.text: Response text proposes updating at least one of the agent docs the probe actually returned
Response text references the new env var name so the doc update is concrete
Produces a `feat(gateway):` commit
Co-Authored-By browzeremb trailer present
